behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam (three source clients -> aggregation/prompt-builder -> summary step). Real failure is missing/dropped source data at handoff across modules, not single-function math. Integration is the minimum that wires collectors plus aggregator together with controlled source responses. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about inclusion of data from all three sources, not real network/OS behavior; in-process fakes can expose omission bugs. Agentic/Workflow rejected because this behavior validates input assembly before model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline in-process with stubbed metrics, issue-tracker, and deployment-log adapters returning distinct sentinel values; assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three sections/keys and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: module/interface seam (source adapter failure -> orchestration error handling -> report renderer). Failure appears when modules interact on exception/partial-data paths. Integration minimum: one adapter throws/unavailable, others succeed, then verify degraded output and non-abort behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real outage characteristics; a deterministic adapter error in-process is sufficient to catch abort-vs-degrade bugs. Agentic/Workflow rejected because this is resilience logic, not model-quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestration modules with one source adapter configured to raise timeout/unavailable, other two returning data; assert run completes, report includes `data unavailable` in only that section, and process exit/status indicates success.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: model-judgment seam (summary correctness depends on real model output quality). Mocked/stubbed summaries would hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors, so Integration is insufficient for this behavior. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior describes one agentic step (summary generation), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed representative input datasets with known key facts, call the real summary model, and score outputs against factual checks (all key values preserved, no contradictions). Run multiple trials with acceptance thresholds to account for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: environment seam (real external APIs + real email delivery/inbox). Real failure boundary is end-to-end infrastructure interaction, especially delivery path to inbox; mocks can pass while SPF/auth/routing/provider API issues fail in reality. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text focuses on successful end-to-end delivery, not quality judgment of model output. Workflow rejected because no multi-agent chain is required by the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scheduled job (or equivalent full run) against real/staging data endpoints and real email service credentials to a test inbox; verify message arrival, recipients, subject/body structure, and included report content sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Seam map: environment seam (email network failure + filesystem persistence + next-run scheduling/process boundary). Minimum real failure boundary crosses OS/filesystem and run-to-run behavior; pure mocks can hide persistence/scheduler defects. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this is retry/persistence orchestration, not multiple agentic model-judgment steps. Agentic rejected because no correctness criterion depends on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: In a system environment, force email service unreachable, run scheduler/job once and verify report is written to expected local path; trigger the next scheduled run with email restored and verify prior report is retried/sent and marked delivered.
